name: PDE5 Class Transfer And Agent Directness Framework Baseline
summary: Stages a targeted baseline framework for deciding when sildenafil, vardenafil, avanafil, mixed PDE5-inhibitor, or class-level evidence may inform tadalafil claims and when it must remain adjacent. The bundle adds a search protocol, six new sources, three artifacts, four findings, and one bounded framework claim.

scope.research_question: When can non-tadalafil PDE5 inhibitor evidence be used to inform tadalafil-focused off-label claims, and what transfer limits must remain explicit?

proposed_changes.summary: Stage targeted search protocol for PDE5 class-transfer framework.
proposed_changes.rationale: The expanded project needs transparent search and source-selection logic for agent-transfer rules.
Sildenafil is the main adjacent PDE5 comparator and needs official pharmacokinetic, selectivity, and safety labeling.
Vardenafil evidence already appears as adjacent cognition evidence and requires current agent-transfer boundaries.

proposed_changes.summary: Add DailyMed STENDRA avanafil label source.
proposed_changes.rationale: Avanafil is an approved ED PDE5 comparator with distinct half-life, nitrate timing, and selectivity language.

proposed_changes.summary: Add NCBI Bookshelf PDE5 inhibitor class overview source.
proposed_changes.rationale: Class mechanism and approved-agent context need a stable source outside individual labels.

proposed_changes.summary: Add comparative PDE5 inhibitor differentiation review source.
proposed_changes.rationale: The review directly discusses mechanism, duration, food effects, and common adverse events across sildenafil, tadalafil, and vardenafil.

proposed_changes.summary: Add comparative PDE selectivity review source.
proposed_changes.rationale: PDE6/PDE11 and other selectivity differences are central limits on class transfer.

proposed_changes.summary: Add comparative label extraction artifact for PDE5 pharmacokinetics and safety boundaries.
proposed_changes.rationale: Agent-transfer rules must preserve drug-specific label differences.

proposed_changes.summary: Add PDE5 mechanism and selectivity extraction artifact.
proposed_changes.rationale: Shared mechanism and off-target selectivity differences define what can and cannot transfer across agents.

proposed_changes.summary: Add project-level PDE5 class-transfer framework artifact.
proposed_changes.rationale: Future synthesis needs reusable rules for tadalafil-direct, adjacent, mixed, and class-level evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding that shared PDE5 mechanism supports plausibility but not outcome equivalence.
proposed_changes.rationale: This prevents class-mechanism evidence from becoming a tadalafil benefit claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding that PDE5 inhibitors are not pharmacokinetically interchangeable for tadalafil claims.
proposed_changes.rationale: Tadalafil's long half-life and daily-use context are central to agent transfer.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding that PDE isoenzyme selectivity differences limit transfer.
proposed_changes.rationale: PDE6/PDE11 and other off-target differences affect mechanism and adverse-effect interpretation.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding that class safety boundaries transfer more reliably than benefit claims but timing remains agent-specific.
proposed_changes.rationale: Shared class contraindications must remain visible without erasing agent-specific nitrate timing.

proposed_changes.summary: Add bounded PDE5 class-transfer framework baseline claim.
proposed_changes.rationale: The broadened project needs a published rule-set before adding more sildenafil, vardenafil, avanafil, or mixed PDE5 evidence.

proposed_claim_implications.note: Creates the first submitted framework for PDE5 class-transfer and agent-directness decisions.
Provides formal context for adjacent vardenafil cognition evidence already published in the cognition baseline.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
endpoint_validity: No correction required; keep outcome-specific endpoints in separate review-question baselines.
endpoint_validity: No correction required; preserve this boundary in public copy.
exposure_directness: No correction required; apply the same metadata discipline to future sildenafil, vardenafil, avanafil, and mixed-PDE5 records.
exposure_directness: Keep exposure qualifiers attached to every future transfer statement.
population_directness: Keep directness language explicit in public synthesis and future outcome-specific baselines.
population_directness: Use this framework as the default, then refine directness during each outcome-specific baseline.
safety_risk_framing: No correction required; keep timing and product-label language agent-specific.
safety_risk_framing: No correction required; retain hard-stop wording near any PDE5 benefit discussion.
source_fidelity: No correction required; re-check label dates during future safety or transfer updates.
source_fidelity: Keep the full-text follow-up actions open before increasing confidence in detailed review-derived transfer rules.
synthesis_overreach: No correction required; use this claim as a gate for future adjacent PDE5 syntheses.
synthesis_overreach: No correction required; maintain this posture in public summaries.
